seo_title: Robinhood lets AI agents trade stocks and make money
meta_description: Robinhood is opening its trading platform to AI agents, allowing users to automate investment decisions with significant risk involved.
body_html:
Direct answer: Robinhood is now allowing users to create a separate account for an AI agent and add a specific amount of money, enabling the agent to buy and sell stocks across the market.

Key Features

  • Users can set up an AI agent with its own trading account and a specific amount of funds.
  • The company pitches the feature as a way for traders to automate investment decisions, such as having an agent monitor specific industries and make trades.

Risk Involved

Agentic trading involves significant risk, including the possible loss of your entire investment. AI-driven strategies may perform poorly under certain market conditions, move quickly, and may be difficult to monitor or stop in real time.

AI agents can be useful for coding, but asking them to buy things on your behalf or fill out online forms often isn’t efficient or accurate.

Technical Details

FeatureDescription
User InterfaceUsers will receive push notifications every time their AI agent makes a trade, view a real-time activity feed in its app, and pause AI trades at any time.
Linking to AI AgentsThe model context protocol (MCP) connects AI systems to apps and data, allowing users to link AI agents to the platform.

source.title: Robinhood will let your AI agent trade stocks and make (or lose) lots of money
source.summary: Robinhood is introducing a new feature that allows users to create a separate trading account for artificial intelligence (AI) agents, enabling these digital entities to trade stocks on their own with a set amount of money. This move aims to expand access to automated trading capabilities for investors. The platform will allow AI agents to buy and sell stocks across the market, potentially generating significant profits or losses.
